question.text: Comparing option exercise styles across derivative markets in India: which is correct?

answer.text: All exchange-traded options in India are European-style — equity (index and stock), currency, and commodity options can only be exercised at expiry
Equity options are American; currency options are European; commodity options vary
All options in India are American-style allowing exercise at any time
Index options are European; all others are American-style

explanation.text: **The Logic:** All exchange-traded options in India are European-style. This includes NIFTY/SENSEX options, stock options, USDINR options, and commodity options. The European style simplifies pricing (Black-Scholes applies directly) and eliminates early assignment risk for sellers. **The Trap:** While stock options COULD be American (as in some other countries), India chose European for all exchange-traded options. OTC options may use either style. **Cross-Exam Context:** This is confirmed across Series VIII (equity), Series I (currency), and Series XVI (commodity). It's a key fact for Series XIII. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** European options mean no early exercise risk — this is particularly beneficial for dividend capture strategies where sellers of American-style calls face early assignment near ex-dividend dates.
